question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send exactly `requestsPerSecond` (default 500) requests per second each with the minimum allowed gas (21,000) to fully exhaust `rateLimitBucket` tokens, causing all other legitimate users to receive `REQUEST_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` ThrottleException and effectively partitioning them from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential check order — `rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1)` before `gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(...)` — by sending requests that pass the rate check but fail the gas check, permanently consuming rate tokens without restoring them, thereby accelerating rate bucket exhaustion and partitioning legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with the maximum allowed gas (15,000,000) to exhaust `gasLimitBucket` tokens (scaled via `scaleGas()`) in a single request, causing all subsequent requests from any user to receive `GAS_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` and partitioning the network for up to one full second?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated `restore()` calls (via crafted requests that hit the `InvalidParametersException` path in `ContractController`) with large gas values, causing `gasLimitBucket.addTokens(tokens)` to push the bucket far beyond its designed capacity, effectively disabling gas throttling and allowing a subsequent flood of high-gas requests to partition the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `rateLimitBucket` is created without an explicit `SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED` (unlike `gasLimitBucket`) to race concurrent requests through the `rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1)` check, allowing more requests than `requestsPerSecond` to pass simultaneously and overwhelming downstream EVM execution, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single request to the opcode endpoint to exhaust `opcodeRateLimitBucket` (default capacity=1), then flood the endpoint with additional requests that all receive `REQUEST_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED`, causing a denial-of-service on the opcode tracing path and partitioning that network segment for one full second?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `ContractCallRequest` with gas just above `GAS_SCALE_FACTOR` (10,000) — e.g., gas=21,000 which scales to 2 tokens — and flood with many such requests to exhaust `gasLimitBucket` at a rate disproportionate to actual EVM cost, causing legitimate high-gas requests to be denied and partitioning high-value users from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `scaleGas()` precision loss — where gas values between 10,001 and 19,999 all scale to 1 token — to send requests consuming only 1 gas token each while still triggering full EVM execution, allowing them to exhaust compute resources before the gas bucket is depleted, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `restore(long gas)` with gas values that, after `scaleGas()`, produce tokens=0 (gas <= 10,000), causing the `gasLimitBucket.addTokens()` call to be skipped entirely, meaning failed requests never restore gas tokens, permanently depleting the gas bucket and partitioning all users from gas-consuming endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `throttle()` iterates ALL matching `requestFilter` entries in `throttleProperties.getRequest()` and calls `action()` for each match, causing multiple LOG actions to fire per request, multiplying log output volume and causing log-based resource exhaustion that partitions the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `scaleGas()` floor division — `Math.floorDiv(gas, 10_000)` — to send requests with gas=10,001 (scaling to 1 token) that pass the gas bucket check while consuming negligible throttle capacity, flooding the system with near-zero-cost requests that exhaust only the rate bucket and partition users via rate limiting?
